title: constants.ts — CARD_REGEX now rejects internal spaces between digit groups
commit_message: fix: require contiguous digits in CARD_REGEX (no spaced groups)

why_card_regex_cascade.explanation: CARD_REGEX lives only in constants.ts. CRITICAL selection is describe('CARD_REGEX') (2 tests). Every other regex describe in standalone/regex.constants.test.js is HIGH (sibling symbols, same file). Cross-dependent rows whose sources[] include constants.ts are also HIGH — same cascade pattern as scenario 2 (PHONE_REGEX) and scenario 13 (EMAIL_REGEX).
why_card_regex_cascade.key_point: 2 CRITICAL + 23 HIGH + 52 SKIP = 77. No feature suite lists CARD_REGEX as a symbol; CARD_REGEX standalone tests are the direct blast centre.

CRITICAL.reason: describe('CARD_REGEX') label exactly matches changed symbol

tests.test: CARD_REGEX > accepts a string of digits and spaces
tests.result: ⚠️  MAY FAIL — if spaced groups like '4111 1111' no longer match

tests.test: CARD_REGEX > rejects letters inside a card number

HIGH.reason: Same source file (constants.ts) — sibling regex describes; plus cross-dependent tests listing constants.ts

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > accepts a valid .com email address
tests.why: Sibling constant in same file — conservative inclusion

tests.test: EMAIL_REGEX > rejects an email address missing the @ symbol
EMAIL_REGEX > rejects a non-.com domain (e.g. .org)
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > accepts a password meeting all requirements
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without an uppercase letter
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password without a special character
PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD > rejects a password longer than 30 characters
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a 10-digit phone number
PHONE_REGEX > accepts a phone number with a + country prefix
PHONE_REGEX > rejects a phone number shorter than 10 digits
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > accepts a valid alphanumeric username with a letter
UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX > rejects a username made entirely of digits
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > accepts a properly spaced 19-character card number
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber contains only digits and spaces
tests.why: sources include constants.ts; CARD_REGEX contract changed

tests.test: generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
storageKeys align with payment flow expectations > storageKeys.stripePaymentDetails equals STRIPEPAYMENTDETAILS
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > accepts a standard 19-character card number with spaces
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number shorter than 17 characters
CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout flow input > rejects a card number that contains letters

SKIP.reason: sources[] have zero overlap with src/types/constants.ts
skipped_files: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js (isUserLoggedIn, updateNewTokenDetails, capitalizeFirstLetter describes — constants.ts not in sources[])
test_repository/cross-dependent/payment-state.cross.test.js (updateUserCards, updatePaymentPlanDetails, paymentActions.CLEARCARDS describes — constants.ts not in sources[])

coverage_gaps.detail: If digits-with-spaces are no longer valid, update standalone expectations and any generateCardNumber assertions that assumed CARD_REGEX allowed spaces.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "CARD_REGEX|EMAIL_REGEX|PASSWORD_REGEX_CHANGE_PASSWORD|PHONE_REGEX|UNIQUE_USERNAME_REGEX|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX|validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|storageKeys align with payment flow|CARD_NUMBER_REGEX validates checkout" --verbose
